message.content:
<deliberation>
I will evaluate each specified GDPR node (the “main_entry_nodes”) against the provided policy chunk. For each node, I will:
- Identify any cross-references contained in that node and list them in "considered_references".
- Look for contradictions or scope differences between the GDPR text (including the referenced nodes where applicable) and the policy chunk.
- Classify only explicit differences as deviations; treat missing/omitted content in the policy as Non-Deviation per instruction 4.
- For each deviation, provide exact GDPR and policy quotes as evidence, a one-sentence mismatch statement, and a short analysis.

Key observations while reviewing the policy:
- The “Right of access” in the policy limits access to data “we store,” whereas GDPR Art 15(1) covers data “being processed.” This matches the provided example of a Data deviation.
- The policy’s right to object is limited to Art 6(1)(f) processing and does not mention Art 6(1)(e) or profiling—this narrows scope compared with Art 21(1).
- The policy’s erasure on consent withdrawal does not include the GDPR qualifier “and where there is no other legal ground,” thereby over-committing (Severity deviation).
- The policy lists only two erasure exceptions (legal obligation and legal claims), omitting other GDPR Art 17(3) exceptions; that narrows the controller’s exemptions and is an over-commitment (Severity deviation).
- Data portability direct transmission (Art 20(2)) in the policy is stated without the “where technically feasible” qualifier—another over-commitment (Severity deviation).

For other nodes (e.g., Arts 13/14 transparency, Art 14(3) timing, Art 18(2) restriction handling, direct marketing objection under Art 21(2), special category data doctors’ secrecy Art 9(3), etc.), the policy chunk is silent; per the instructions, these are treated as Non-Deviation due to omission.

I will now provide the JSON results.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-021",
    "clause": "Art15(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-041"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Data deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller confirmation as to whether or not personal data concerning him or her are being processed",
        "policy_quote": "Right of access: You can request information from us at any time about whether and which personal data we store about you.",
        "mismatch": "The GDPR covers access to personal data being processed, while the policy limits the right to data the controller stores.",
        "analysis": "By limiting the scope to data ‘we store,’ the policy narrows the GDPR scope that covers all processing states (not just storage)."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-031",
    "clause": "Art20(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-009"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-035",
    "clause": "Art21(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Data deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "The data subject shall have the right to object, on grounds relating to his or her particular situation, at any time to processing ... which is based on point (e) or (f) of Article 6(1), including profiling based on those provisions.",
        "policy_quote": "Right to object to processing: If the processing of your data is based on Art 6 para 1 lit f) GDPR, you may object to the processing at any time.",
        "mismatch": "The policy limits the objection right to Art 6(1)(f) processing, whereas the GDPR includes both Art 6(1)(e) and (f) and explicitly includes profiling.",
        "analysis": "The scope is narrowed by excluding public interest/official authority processing and profiling; this is a substantive limit compared to GDPR."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-020",
    "clause": "Art14(5)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-098", "REG-016"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-010",
    "clause": "Art9(3)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-009", "REG-008"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-018",
    "clause": "Art14(3)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-016", "REG-017"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-092",
    "clause": "Art77(1)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-028",
    "clause": "Art18(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-035"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-025",
    "clause": "Art17(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-009", "REG-035", "REG-036", "REG-005"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "(b) the data subject withdraws consent on which the processing is based according to point (a) of Article 6(1), or point (a) of Article 9(2), and where there is no other legal ground for the processing;",
        "policy_quote": "Right to erasure: You have the right to request that we erase your personal data if and to the extent that the data is no longer needed for the purposes for which it was collected or, if the processing is based on your consent, you have withdrawn your consent.",
        "mismatch": "The policy grants erasure upon withdrawal of consent without the GDPR qualifier that there must be no other legal ground for processing.",
        "analysis": "By omitting the ‘no other legal ground’ condition, the policy commits to a broader erasure right than required (over-compliance)."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-027",
    "clause": "Art17(3)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-009", "REG-010", "REG-098"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "Paragraphs 1 and 2 shall not apply to the extent that processing is necessary: (a) for exercising the right of freedom of expression and information; (b) for compliance with a legal obligation ... or for the performance of a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority ...; (c) for reasons of public interest in the area of public health in accordance with points (h) and (i) of Article 9(2) as well as Article 9(3); (d) for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) ...; or (e) for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims.",
        "policy_quote": "A right to erasure does not exist insofar as • the data may not be deleted due to a legal obligation or must be processed due to a legal obligation; • the data processing is necessary for the assertion, exercise or defence of legal claims.",
        "mismatch": "The policy lists only two exceptions to erasure (legal obligation and legal claims), omitting other GDPR exceptions such as freedom of expression, public interest/official authority, public health, and archiving.",
        "analysis": "By presenting a narrower set of exemptions, the policy over-commits to erasure compared to GDPR (which allows additional lawful derogations)."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-029",
    "clause": "Art18(2)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-032",
    "clause": "Art20(2)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "the data subject shall have the right to have the personal data transmitted directly from one controller to another, where technically feasible.",
        "policy_quote": "Right to data portability: You have the right to receive from us the data you have provided in a structured, common and machine-readable format, as well as the right to have this data transferred to another controller.",
        "mismatch": "The policy grants a right to direct transmission without conditioning it on technical feasibility as required by the GDPR.",
        "analysis": "Omitting the ‘where technically feasible’ qualifier broadens the controller’s obligation beyond GDPR’s limit (over-compliance)."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-013",
    "clause": "Art13(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-009", "REG-041"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-036",
    "clause": "Art21(2)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-017",
    "clause": "Art14(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-009", "REG-041"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  }
]
```
